title: Austerity of Body MInd & Words | Bhagavad Gita 17.14 - 17.16 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So today we'll be covering three verses from the 17th chapter, 14 to 17, based on
the teachings of Srila Prabhupada. Text 14. The austerity of body consists in this
worship of the supreme Lord, the Brahmanas, the spiritual master and superiors like
father and mother, cleanliness, simplicity, celibacy and non-violence are also
austerities of the body. What do we mean by austerity? Osterity of the body,
austerity of... Purity is not the right meaning, although in one sense, yes, what you are saying
is right, in one sense, yes. Control is also not the literal meaning. What does austerity
mean? No. Tapas, austere, he is very austere, he is very, very regulated, he is very, very
self-controlled. So what is that austerity? What is tapas for mind? What is tapas for
body? What it may not like doing it, but it should do. Getting up early in the morning
is austerity. Isn't it? Cozy bed, to get out of the bed is an austerity. We may not like
chanting initially, to do it is an austerity. It's a tapas.
So austerity means tapas. So here in these verses, Krishna is mentioning, what is the
austerity for the body? What is the austerity for the mind? Austerity for the senses. So
Krishna is saying, worship of the Supreme Lord is the austerity for the body. When we
Desire of the body is to worship itself, is to glorify itself, is to fulfill the demands
of itself. That is the natural instinct. So when we say worship of the Lord, that's why
Prabhupada instituted Deity worship all over the world, many, many temples, where everyone
has a desire to dress himself nicely, have a desire to eat some nice foodstuff. How we
can dovetail that desire and see the beautiful forms of the Lord. How we can dress the Lord
beautifully. So worshipping of the Supreme Lord, it can happen even in our houses. It's
an austerity of the body. Worship of the Brahmanas. Why Brahmanas? Because they are disseminating
the knowledge about the Lord in the society.
when we use the word Brahmanas, especially in our history books, we have studied about
the caste system. Brahmanas, Kshatriyas, Vaishyas, Shudras and it was considered as an evil in
the society, social evil. And from this caste system came the evil of untouchability, Dalits.
And even in India, there are some remote parts, there are some places where there are some
parts where still that untouchability is practiced where in a particular village,
there is separate village for the higher caste, there is separate village for the lower castes.
And people call their Dalits Achut or they cannot come to the temple. And that's how
the different social reformers came and they tried to dismantle this so-called caste system
in India. But Krishna in the Gita says,
Krishna is saying, I have created this caste system.
So how can Lord create a caste system which is considered as an evil in the society,
which has led to so-called untouchability, so-called discrimination basis,
caste, color, creed. How can Krishna advocate a system like that?
So Krishna says in the Gita, anyone can tell me what is the right understanding of caste system?
Chatur varna maya shrishta. Maya shrishta means I have created.
How? On what basis? Guna, karma, vibhagasha.
Prominently in sattva guna, in raja guna, in tamo guna and what kind of work he is engaged in.
Now the so-called evil came in the society when people who were born in brahmana family
started claiming that I am a brahmana, so you should respect me.
It is like, it is like judge of a high court judge, his son claiming that I am also a judge.
Or it is also like a doctor who is a big surgeon and his son saying I am also a surgeon.
No, you can't be a surgeon by birth.
If you qualify, if you go to the required qualification courses, yes, you can be also a surgeon.
Now this whole evil came in the society when people started claiming that I am a brahmana, you are a shudra.
the instruction given by different religious institutions in??
The education, the institutions, what is it?
It is just the instruction that brings money and even their salary.
It doesn't matter what it is that we do and it adds up to a huge amount over time.
Yajna means to please the Supreme Lord.
So, a person who is engaged in these activities, irrespective of where he has taken birth, is considered a Ramana.
For example, now, when we, assuming that we have to undergo an operation,
let's say we go to the hospital, Hirandani hospital, and we want to get operated, we have some serious ailment.
Now, our consideration while deciding a doctor is not the caste, it's not the religion.
If suppose one, Mr. Joseph Murray is a good cardiologist, we don't mind getting operated from,
because he has the required qualification.
So, such Brahmanas, that's why in Vedic times, even in our customs, it's a custom that Brahmanas are called,
to the house, they are invited, their feet is washed.
Or Brahman Bhojan, you know, sometimes when there is ceremony in the family, the Brahmanas are invited and they are fed.
It is said that, the Lord eats through the mouth of Brahmanas.
Now, the evil came in the society when people started demanding respect,
even though they did not have the required qualification.
they were born in a family of a Brahmana.
So, here austerity of the body is worship of Brahmanas.
in touch with the Lord, who are
broadcasting the glories of the Lord,
who are pure, such souls have to be also worshipped.
Worship of the spiritual master, Guru.
spiritual master is important? Anyone can help me with that? Why it is important?
We cannot approach the Supreme Lord directly. Yasya Prasadat Bhagwat Prasado.
If we get the mercy of a pure devotee of the Lord, we can get the mercy of Lord.
Yasya Prasadat Nagati Kutopi. If you do not get the mercy of a pure devotee, there is no hope for us.
Even in material mundane sense, if we have to meet a big man,
although analogy has limitation, even if you have to meet a big man, you have to go through
some proper channel. Potentially, yes, we can meet Narendra Modi, but we can't pick up a phone and
say, I want to come and meet you. Can you give me an appointment? You have to find a person who is
very close to him, meet him. Can you please organize a meeting? Yes, I will organize.
Somebody who's already close to him. You all agree with that? If you have to meet somebody big,
although it's a crude analogy, it has limitation, but that's how it works. So a brahmana, a
spiritual master, who is very, very dear to Lord, is a confidential servitor of the Lord.
He understands the mood of the Lord. He is serving the Lord.
Such a spiritual master also has to be worshipped.
Superiors like father and mother. In Indian custom, we have Indian tradition, it's so nice.
westernization and the traditions have so-called dismantled or eroded to some extent,
still we have a concept of touching the feet of parents.
Off-lit, have you seen the bend has reduced a little bit?
Have you seen that? So the more we are getting, so the bend has reduced.
So what if the father and mother, you are practicing the principles of Gita,
chanting Hare Krishna Mantra, but parents are saying this is all nonsense, why do you do all this?
No, but here it says we should worship the father and mother.
That scenario is possible, yes or no? You agree what I'm saying? A scenario where
you are doing something which you know is right, chanting the names of the Lord, reading the Gita,
and sometimes parents may say, why are you wasting time? So should we respect and obey them? Yes, no?
How many of you say yes?
They say, don't go to the temple.
How many of you say yes? They say, don't go to the temple.
Don't go to the Gita class, they will brainwash you.
You have to convince why we are going. How many of us say we can disobey them in such cases?
But you are going against the instructions. They are superiors and they have to be respected.
They have to be obeyed. But they are saying, don't chant. They are saying, don't read Gita.
I remember, there was a boy who wanted to join a mission. He was about 22 years old.
And when he shared his news with his parents, his family members and all, they were quite upset and against his joining.
So, when he was trying to convince them, so this grandfather came with his grandson who was
And he was very upset, very angry. He says,
What have you done to him?
I have not started reading Gita till today.
And he is trying to read Gita at the age of 18, 20.
Seventy, look at me, I am 70 years.
I have not started Gita at that age of 20.
He was in some kind of accomplishment.
He said, may look at me I have not started reading Gita at that age.
And this fellow 20 years old he wants to read Gita
So, we have examples of all those superiors have to be respected.
In fact, they have to be worshipped.
So, we have examples of all those superiors have to be respected. In fact, they have to be worshipped.
Although superiors have to be respected, In fact, they have to be worshiped.
But we also have examples of great devotees who disobeyed their superiors.
Can anyone give me an example?
Prahlad Maharaj, his father was Hrini Kashyapu.
He was very much against worship of Vishnu.
And Prahlad Maharaj is said to be Mahajana.
Mahajana means authority in spiritual science.
If we have to understand religious principles, we have to follow in the footsteps of great Mahajanas.
How they acted, by their actions we can ascertain what are religious principles.
So we have example of Prahlad Maharaj who disobeyed his father.
He disobeyed and he is respected as Prahlad Maharaj.
Another example, we are talking about spiritual master.
He was the guru of Bali Maharaj.
He was the guru of Bali Maharaj.
He was the guru of Bali Maharaj.
Bali Maharaj have you heard of?
Don't tell me, disciple of Shukracharya.
So Bali Maharaj, you know after Diwali we have Bali Paadmi or we have Vaman Jayanti,
that is the festival observed for this particular pastime.
So Bali Maharaj was a great king.
But he was born in Asura family, but still he was a great devotee of the Lord.
So one time, Lord Vishnu came at his doorstep dressed as a Brahmana.
What is the name of the Brahmana? Vamanadev. And just like, you know when Brahmans come
or something, we give something in charity. They ask something and we say this thing.
So this Brahman boy said that I don't want anything, I just want three pieces of land.
Three pieces means the land on which I can step, three steps of land. So the Shukracharya who was
so-called very intelligent, he understood that this is Vishnu has come in disguise. He is calling
for three steps, but actually he will take away everything. And if he takes away everything, my
Because all his source of income, whatever it is, this thing is coming from
Bali Maharaj. So everyone is a little selfish in that aspect. So he said don't give. He is Vishnu
himself who has come in disguise. Don't give, don't give. He is asking for three pieces. Don't give.
So Bali Maharaj said you only have told, no, that Vishnu is supreme.
And goal of life is to surrender to Vishnu. If Vishnu himself has come and he is asking,
what's wrong in it if I give?
So much against the wish of Shukracharya, Bali Maharaj agreed to give three pieces of
land to Bhamanadev. And that's why Bali Maharaj is also regarded, respected as a great devotee.
So to cut the story short, he took one step, he covered the entire material universe. The second
The outer universe, the entire so-called spiritual, this thing. And he said, where do I keep the third step? He says, keep on my head. I am yours.

So, yes, we have to respect our parents. Yes, we have to respect our elders. But sometimes they can be, because everyone has individual soul. And even today, the so-called parents could be spoiled children of yesterday.
You know, slowly the culture is eroding. These days we see a lot of

so-called young people having a lot of bad habits. They're smoking, they're drinking, they're womanizing, they're doing all kinds of things. They're going to be parents for another ten years, five years.
So we cannot just blindly take a statement that you have to respect our parents. We have to use our intelligence. Yes, the culture is we have to respect our parents.
But if you are very sure what they are saying is against scriptural injunctions,
devotees that they have disobeyed their parents also.

The bank had to pay something for having conducted this. So it is happening at a very,
very... the person who is doing it also is doing with material motive. The person who
is actually getting into this occupation also is purely because it is a source of revenue
for him. The whole original intent of thing is lost. It is a profession. It fetches their
money. They do a yajna and gets 4000 rupees, 2000 rupees. And the person who does it also,
He has no intent of pleasing the Lord.
Yajna is not done for that.
Dramanas are not meant to be used for this.
So the whole, in Kali Yuga, the whole equation is that way very deteriorated.
Worship of spiritual masters, superiors like father and mother.
Yes, we have to respect them.
But there have been occasions where great devotees have even disobeyed their fathers
and it is considered to be okay.
So then austerity of the body also includes practicing,
practice cleansing the body externally and internally.

Internal cleanliness, how does it come about?
So internal cleanliness happens by chanting, by remembering the Lord.
So practice cleansing, cheto, darpanam, marjanam, bhava maha, davagni, nirvapanam.
Cheto, our heart which is now impure, which is covered with the dust.
We are not able to see ourself.
This chanting process cleanses the mirror of our heart.
Assuming you have a mirror in your this thing and you open the house after three years,
The mirror would have been coated with a layer of dust.
And you will not be able to see properly.
And you wipe that mirror, you cleanse the mirror, you will be able to see properly.
Right now, our state is like that.
A heart, the mirror of a heart is covered with a layer of dust.
What is the dust? Dust of?
Dust of ignorance, dust of materialism.

bhava maha davagni, because of this dust,
because of this material thing which has, so to say, glued on to us,
bhava maha davagni, we are the blazing fire of material existence.
Just like in a forest, it's a forest fire.
Likewise, we are also burning in the fire of material existence.
So when chanting happens, it cleanses the heart
We get relief from the blazing fire of material existence.
